transcript: Alright guys, we are here with a 2005 Honda Element. As you can see, the window is broken. We're going to learn how to replace it. This is the tool I'm going to use. I'm going to use a drill with a Phillips bit, a ratchet with a 10 millimeter socket and an extension, a tack puller, and an adapter for the drill and the window. First of all we are going to remove the cover using the tack puller, we are going to start with that one, and then remove the other cover, then unscrew the door handle, then remove the speaker cover, unscrew the speaker, and then unplug it. Now we start removing the door panel, it's easy, all you have to do is use the tack puller in the bottom half. And then we're going to leave it plugged, roll down the window. There are two holes. And then make sure you align it right there. And then I'm going to use the 10mm socket with the drill. It's just two bolts, so now I'm going to take the second bolt down, and I'm going to hold the window if it's going to fall and it's going to break in pieces, it's going to be harder to take it out. So once it's released, all you got to do is take it out to Be careful. Whew. There you go. Alright, this is the new window. Basically, you are going to see there are two holes right there. We've got to line it up on those holes on the door. And what we've got gotta do is slide the window and then place it on where it goes make sure then line it up with the holes and then screw it using the drill and make sure they are a lot of time it out you can do it or you can do it using a ratchet and a 10 millimeter socket Roll down and roll it up several times and that means it's good to go. Alright all you got to do is put everything back on and this is how it's going to look. So now you know how to replace a broken window in a 2005 Honda Element. Thanks for watching, have a nice day
